name: Gacha Nexus RPG (DeepSeek V3 coded)
description:
Welcome to My First Gacha RPG!

This project is still in Alpha, so please let me know if you run into any issues or have feedback. My goal is to make this an engaging, fun experience for everyone.

About the Character Generator

The heart of this Gacha system is its advanced character generator — every pull creates a complete character, not just stats and art.
Each character comes with:

  • Race – 38 total

  • Class – 27 total

  • Personality Archetype – 10 total

  • Motivation – 8 total

  • Quirks – 6 total

The system combines these elements to generate unique characters with depth, personality, and story potential.

Getting Started – Recommended

  • Use a persona: The GM will use your persona as a base to determine your class and race unless you specifically state otherwise.

  • Use Auto-Summary as a checkpoint every 8–12 turns, or after a rank-up/quest clear, to keep things consistent.

    Deepseek V3 update: You can now create your own origin to start from a custom scenario instead of the default beginning.

    Here is the beginners guide for how everything works:

    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/justcheckin345/Gacha-RPG-Guide./main/assets/GachaRPG_Beginners_Guide_v1.3.pdf

    Includes the full race/class lists and stat mapping notes for personas.

Gacha XP Costs

  • F Class Fate (Common)– 100 XP

  • D Class Fate (Uncommon) – 250 XP

  • C Class Fate (Rare) – 500 XP

  • B Class Fate (Epic) – 1,000 XP

  • A Class Fate (Legendary) – 2,000 XP

  • S Class Fate (Mythic) – 5,000 XP

Core Features

1. Vast, In-Depth Character Gacha Generator

  • Every pull generates a full backstory, personality profile, quirks, faction ties, and unique motivations.

  • NPCs feel alive — they build grudges, form crushes, and react to events dynamically.

  • Choices matter: NPCs can become allies, rivals, love interests, or enemies.

2. Diablo-Style Loot System

  • Procedurally generated loot with rarity tiers, affixes, and synergy bonuses.

  • Weapons and gear are tied to world lore — e.g., finding a Phoenix Fang Dagger might pull you into that faction’s politics.

  • Loot can alter story arcs if it supports or conflicts with a character’s goals.

3. Fully Reactive GM Logic

  • Story reacts to actions, tone, alliances, and moral choices.

  • NPCs remember past interactions — even across arcs.

  • Your choices ripple forward, shaping future pulls and loot drops.

4. Personality-Aware Dialogue

  • Every NPC has a unique speech style.

  • Their tone shifts based on trust, fear, or admiration toward you.

  • Banter and conflict feel organic — no generic quest text.
creator: jeosjne
